| 20 | [↗](https://woogles.io/game/cSKWCP9mERHovKo9vB7UoZ) | Sujana Somonasekar | W | 520 | 423 | +97 | 1.0 | 3 | 0 | 2 | 1 | 3 | 3.0% | solid win |

## Missed Bingos

*Lowercase = blank tile; (X) = board tile X was already there*

| Rd | Opponent | Word |
|---|---|---|
| 3 | Komol | cOEXtEN(D) |
| 8 | Sam Crispin | R(E)EX(P)oSED |

| 11 | Austin Shin | CHATLIN(E) |
| 13 | Charnwit Sukhumrattanaporn | (B)AReHEAD |
| 14 | Josh Castellano | (T)ARTARIC |
| 14 | Josh Castellano | b(O)TONNEE |
| 15 | Soramet | REGINAE |
| 18 | Chollapat | IN(T)ERIO(R)S |

## Summary
A tough 9–10 result against a strong field. Win% lost tells a sharper story than the record alone: the two cruelest losses (Round 18, −3 vs Chollapat and Round 19, −2 vs Odette Rio) had win% lost of 31.7% and 48.3% respectively — Jesse was steadily leaking equity in both, not just getting unlucky at the end. Round 19's 48.3% is the highest of either tournament analyzed and aligns with the 4.1 mistakes index (also the worst of the event). On the positive side, the dominant stretch through Rounds 6–11 showed consistently low win% lost (4–11%), capped by a monster Round 11 (605–445 vs Austin Shin at just 5.7%). The bingo find rate of 82.0% (41/50 opportunities, after removing one false-positive in R2) and a missed-bingo rate of one every 2.1 games suggest spotting sevens and eights was a meaningful area of leakage. Blanks drawn were below average at 0.79/game.

| 1 | [↗](https://woogles.io/game/hfaeKGQyoiBihz3EmX3JQe) | Michael Donegan | W | 482 | 296 | +186 | 0.6 | 2 | 0 | 1 | 0 | 0 | 1.5% | very clean |
| 2 | [↗](https://woogles.io/game/3bqJ9CQ5vfyxnzra2efNZD) | Anuj Shetty | W | 547 | 362 | +185 | 1.7 | 3 | 0 | 1 | 1 | 12 | 1.8% | +185 dominant; Anuj Shetty phony GU/PU* |
| 3 | [↗](https://woogles.io/game/RXjof385YpJwDEPZeX2kgy) | James Curley | W | 395 | 359 | +36 | 3.4 | 2 | 2 | 2 | 2 | 19 | 29.0% | phony LINUX/NEEL* (unchallenged); missed bingos #1–#2 (COURAN(T)O, DOGGEDl(Y)) |
| 4 | [↗](https://woogles.io/game/FT7z7zHdXmMXxgtgRKSWpH) | Becky Dyer | W | 524 | 452 | +72 | 3.1 | 2 | 0 | 3 | 1 | 62 | 5.1% | 62-pt endgame |
| 5 | [↗](https://woogles.io/game/SjBZWqeRda545PuojvRAUM) | Chris Canik | L | 384 | 437 | −53 | 2.3 | 1 | 0 | 3 | 1 | 0 | 35.2% | competitive |
| 6 | [↗](https://woogles.io/game/4CEbb26JnESdQQCSDSaggG) | Becky Dyer | W | 497 | 423 | +74 | 0.8 | 2 | 0 | 3 | 1 | 7 | 2.4% | very clean |
| **Avg** | | | | **471.5** | **388.2** | **+83.3** | **1.98** | **2.00** | **0.33** | **2.17** | **1.00** | **16.7** | **12.5%** | |

## Missed Bingos

*Lowercase = blank tile; (X) = board tile X was already there*

| Rd | Opponent | Word |
|---|---|---|
| 3 | James Curley | COURAN(T)O |
| 3 | James Curley | DOGGEDl(Y) |

## Summary
A dominant one-day performance with a 5–1 record and an average winning margin of +83 points. The cleanest games bookended the day — Rounds 1 and 6 had win% lost of just 1.5% and 2.4%, both against familiar opponents. The sole loss came in Round 5 against Chris Canik (who bingoed three times), and Jesse gave away 35.2% of win probability that game — the highest of the tournament, suggesting the deficit was built steadily rather than from a single blunder. Round 3 was the outlier on the winning side: a phony played and two missed bingos (COURANTO and DOGGEDLY) contributed to a 29% win% lost despite a comfortable result. The biggest cautionary stat is Round 4 — a win over Becky Dyer by 72 points that still left 62 endgame spread points behind, primarily from failing to play out a lone L tile while Becky held an unplayable Q.
